Leptocarpus is a genus of Australian rush plants, belonging to the family Restionaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of infections. Leptocarpus is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, with the most common preparations being used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
